Triggering the WFOE (Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise) Sales Office precision validation probe. The fabricated Apple global supply chain OEM template in the original data has been physically eradicated. This enterprise, named 'Aircom Automotive Technology,' was extremely exaggeratedly marked as 'Complete Automobile Manufacturing' in the official classification, and its business license indeed encompasses a massive manufacturing category ranging from 'air suspension devices' to 'medical devices.' However, the forensic logic engine captured its extremely unique data combination: First, the enterprise type is 'sole proprietorship by foreign legal person,' and the legal representative is a foreign national; second, the paid-in capital reaches a staggering 16.58 million RMB; but shockingly, its insured headcount is only 1 person! The registered address is 'No. A-172, 219 Lantian Road, Lukou Subdistrict, Jiangning District' (a typical virtual concentrated registration address). In China's commercial soil, 'wholly foreign-owned + multi-million massive paid-in capital + very few employees + affiliated address' is a very classic configuration: it is not a physical manufacturing factory, but a 'WFOE Sales Office / Commercial Representative Office' set up in China by a multinational group (identifiable via its email suffix `aircom.ag` as the European pneumatic system giant Aircom). This 1 employee is highly likely its chief commercial representative in China. The multi-million paid-in capital is the funding pool for the foreign enterprise to conduct bulk import and export trade in China. It does not build cars, nor does it start production of air suspensions in China; its core mission is to sell the core components manufactured overseas by its parent company (Aircom), such as automotive pneumatic valves and tire repair kits, to Chinese new energy vehicle enterprises through 'import and export business,' and to provide technical support. The system has corrected its absurd 'complete vehicle manufacturing plant' tag, accurately resetting it as the 'sales branch of a multinational automotive component giant in China.'
